    <p>{`A brief history of our terminology`}</p>
    <p>{`Over the years, Adobe Creative Cloud applications have supported ExtendScript `}<strong parentName="p">{`Scripts`}</strong>{`, Flash `}<strong parentName="p">{`Panels`}</strong>{`, CEP `}<strong parentName="p">{`Extensions`}</strong>{`, UXP `}<strong parentName="p">{`Plugins`}</strong>{` (either `}<em parentName="p">{`regular`}</em>{` or `}<em parentName="p">{`hybrid`}</em>{`), and UXP `}<strong parentName="p">{`Scripts`}</strong>{`.     <p>{`While this continues to be the case, Premiere will use `}<strong parentName="p">{`plugins`}</strong><sup parentName="p" {...{

Primarily, this is to maintain consistency with the other Adobe Creative Cloud applications that have migrated—or are about to migrate—to the new UXP standard.`}</p>
